opp.ais_why:
• Across the 89-minute 2/13/2026 demo, Pep stakeholders Tim Drost, Jillian Lushman, and Christina Behm did not voice a single AI, Merlin, Agentic, ANA, Intake, or GenAI reference. The full conversation revolved around iSupplier, iContract, and iRisk workflows: onboarding, due diligence, contract review, and supplier risk integrations with EcoVadis and DNB.
• Buyer questions were process-mechanical: white-labelling, conditional questioning, delegation and impersonation, predetermined categories, and IT questionnaire integrations. Tim's framing question was "How do you guys bundle this thing up to sell it?" focused on commercial packaging. Even with 89 minutes on the clock, no one asked about agents or Merlin Intake.
• Confirmed next steps lock the direction: selection of iContract, iSupplier, and iRisk modules, then literature and a quote. That matches the original scope of iSupplier, iRisk Lite, and iContract. No POC, no AI line items, and no champion advocating for AI capability.

champion.narrative: Christina Behm is a genuine working champion but mid-level and now in an approvals holding pattern. Tim Drost is the higher-power potential sponsor but disengaged since Dec. Single-threaded with no escalation path. Arming Christina with a value case AND broadening to Tim is the champion move.

competition.narrative: Primary alternative is internal build (extend Pep Connect), kept alive by Tim ('it still could happen'), backed by 20+ developers. Our wedge — speed-to-value, freeing engineers for client work, native Merlin AI depth — is real but not yet in a written buy-vs-build case for the EB.

identify_pain.narrative: Specific, owner-attributed, rising. Exceptions 'live in Jillian's head'; Tim: 'visibility into what the agreements might say... is probably one of our biggest pain points.'

paper_process.narrative: NDA signed; implementation scoped 10-20 weeks. No MSA/SOW, legal path, procurement channel or InfoSec timeline discussed, and no contract-expiration forcing function (net-new vs internal build). Surfacing the buyer's legal/procurement/InfoSec sequence is needed to make any close date real.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer engaged. Tim Drost (VP Sourcing Ops, Christina's manager) validated need on 17 Dec 2025 and is the closest budget signal, but has not driven the deal since and is unconfirmed as budget holder. The $120k has sat in an opaque 'internal approvals' process since 26 May. Arming Christina to broker a Tim conversation — not cold-bypassing her — is the path to power. Until a budget owner validates the spend, the Upside Key Deal forecast is indefensible.

decision_process.narrative: Process mapped through Feb 2026, then internal. Path beyond approvals (pricing acceptance, on-site, InfoSec, contracting) undefined with the buyer; a written mutual close plan is the gap.

decision_criteria.narrative: Well documented and aligned to iSupplier/iContract/iRisk; AI/Merlin appetite explicit.

competitors.name: Internal Build (extend Pep Connect system)
competitors.quote: We looked at building this, and for my comments earlier, it still could happen. We wanna focus on our clients and building stuff for our clients and not on this. Not that it's not built. It's just not, you know, I think you get it. So this is not like a warm and fuzzy let's learn more. We truly are engaging a number of folks and gonna make a decision here on a path forward.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 17 Dec 2025, Tim Drost (VP Sourcing Operations)
competitors.how_we_win: Speed to value (Zycus 10-20 weeks vs internal build 12-18 months), engineering focus (keep Pep's 20+ developers on client-facing AI and campaign features, not plumbing), and AI depth (Merlin risk scoring, OCR document extraction, clause intelligence, which Pep cannot build without heavy ML investment). Activate the wedge by building a quantified buy-vs-build business case (cost of internal build = dev hours + opportunity cost of not building client features + 12-18 month delay vs Zycus $120k + 10-20 week implementation + immediate ROI on manual-process elimination and exception-tracking automation), present it to the EB with Tim's executive sponsorship, and force a vendor-selection decision before third-week-of-July reconnect.

competitors.name: Other third-party vendor platforms (unnamed)
competitors.quote: We truly are engaging a number of folks and gonna make a decision here on a path forward.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 17 Dec 2025, Tim Drost (VP Sourcing Operations); SF Next_Step__c 17 Dec 2025 'evaluating multiple vendors'
competitors.how_we_win: No competitor names or sentiment visible across 4 calls and 7 months, which suggests either Pep is tight-lipped or Zycus is the only external platform being seriously evaluated. If other vendors exist, Zycus wins by demonstrating tighter fit to Pep's workflow (scenario session 13 Feb validated exact onboarding process), deeper AI capabilities (Merlin suite), and faster implementation. If Zycus is sole external platform, the real competition is the internal build, not another vendor.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / slow decision
competitors.quote: We also don't have a timeline... it's not urgent.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 17 Dec 2025, Christina Behm (Sourcing Operations Manager)
competitors.how_we_win: Create urgency by quantifying the cost of inaction (manual onboarding continues to block fast client engagements per Tim 17 Dec 'program launches in 3 weeks, forced to make exceptions'; exception tracking stays in buyers' heads, creating single-point-of-failure risk if Christina or Jillian leave; expired COIs and agreements create compliance and audit exposure per Jillian 17 Dec). Tie urgency to a business event (new fiscal year starting 1 Jul 2026, new client onboarding wave, compliance audit, leadership visibility into supplier risk). Without urgency, deal drifts indefinitely.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong fit on the confirmed scope (iSupplier, iContract, iRisk); the demo and scenario session directly addressed Pep's exception-tracking, expiry-alert, role-based-visibility and conditional-questionnaire requirements.

hard.ais_why:
• Across the 89-minute 2/13/2026 demo, Pep stakeholders Tim Drost, Jillian Lushman, and Christina Behm did not voice a single AI, Merlin, Agentic, ANA, Intake, or GenAI reference. The full conversation revolved around iSupplier, iContract, and iRisk workflows: onboarding, due diligence, contract review, and supplier risk integrations with EcoVadis and DNB.
• Buyer questions were process-mechanical: white-labelling, conditional questioning, delegation and impersonation, predetermined categories, and IT questionnaire integrations. Tim's framing question was "How do you guys bundle this thing up to sell it?" focused on commercial packaging. Even with 89 minutes on the clock, no one asked about agents or Merlin Intake.
• Confirmed next steps lock the direction: selection of iContract, iSupplier, and iRisk modules, then literature and a quote. That matches the original scope of iSupplier, iRisk Lite, and iContract. No POC, no AI line items, and no champion advocating for AI capability.

Close date 28 Jul 2026 is 32 days out but buyer is in internal approvals (status as of 26 May) and asked to reconnect third week of July (after close date per 15 Jun Next_Step). Remaining steps (EB engagement, final pricing, contracting, InfoSec, signature) require 8-12 weeks minimum. Date will slip 6-10 weeks.

Economic Buyer unidentified and never engaged across 7+ months and four buyer calls. No CFO, CEO, or budget authority has validated the $120k spend or business case. Until EB engages and confirms investment, deal cannot progress past Shortlisted to contracting. Upside Key Deal forecast is indefensible without EB sign-off. Next move: when Christina reconnects third week of July, ask directly who will approve the $120k spend, request an introduction, and schedule an executive briefing (business case, ROI, reference customers) within two weeks.

Single-threaded on Christina Behm (Decision Maker, operational champion). Tim Drost (VP Sourcing Ops, Christina's manager) joined 17 Dec discovery and has been cc'd but is not actively driving the deal. Jillian Lushman (Sourcing Lead, Evaluator) is engaged but not a power player. If Christina is unavailable or stalls in approvals, deal has no secondary path forward. Risk: Christina is in approval-hold since 26 May (31 days) and deferred reconnection to third week July (after 28 Jul close date). Next move: when reconnecting with Christina, request a joint call with Tim to maintain senior-level momentum and begin Economic Buyer access path. In parallel, Grace or Michael McCarthy (deal owner's manager) should connect directly with Tim to multi-thread at VP level.

Internal build alternative is still live and is the primary competitive threat. Tim stated 17 Dec 'it still could happen' and Pep has 20+ developers with build capability. Zycus wedge exists (speed, engineering focus, AI sophistication) but has not yet been activated with the Economic Buyer or formalized in a written business case that positions buy vs build. Risk: if budget pressure mounts or approvals stall, Pep may default to a slow internal build rather than buying Zycus. Next move: when reconnecting third week of July, surface the build-vs-buy decision explicitly, ask who is championing the build internally, and present a one-page business case (speed: 10-20 weeks vs 6-12 month build; cost: implementation + license vs internal dev burn + opportunity cost; risk: proven platform vs unproven first build) to tip the decision to the relevant stakeholder this as the Economic Buyer presentation anchor.

value.name: Internal Build (extend Pep Connect system)
value.quote: We looked at building this, and for my comments earlier, it still could happen. We wanna focus on our clients and building stuff for our clients and not on this. Not that it's not built. It's just not, you know, I think you get it. So this is not like a warm and fuzzy let's learn more. We truly are engaging a number of folks and gonna make a decision here on a path forward.

value.how_we_win: Speed to value (Zycus 10-20 weeks vs internal build 12-18 months), engineering focus (keep Pep's 20+ developers on client-facing AI and campaign features, not plumbing), and AI depth (Merlin risk scoring, OCR document extraction, clause intelligence, which Pep cannot build without heavy ML investment). Activate the wedge by building a quantified buy-vs-build business case (cost of internal build = dev hours + opportunity cost of not building client features + 12-18 month delay vs Zycus $120k + 10-20 week implementation + immediate ROI on manual-process elimination and exception-tracking automation), present it to the EB with Tim's executive sponsorship, and force a vendor-selection decision before third-week-of-July reconnect.
